whenever you can, you want to stack them up in both draw and discard piles, when you exalt with aurita it consumes them for extra hits, and at lvl5 soulforge for each one she consumes this way it gives her bonus % damage for the rest of the battle. More over on boss battles after 20 of them are consumed this way. All gland divusions cause a bonus hit, when used or consumed

I guess the normal play is just Corpo exalt into her exalt with as many divison in hand as possible is the easiest combo
you can see how this rolls out of control AFTER
also, if you have them in hand, when you discard glands they give aurita alimus
so there is some balancing between using, holding, discarding, and consuming with her exalt

Isnt going to 100 CR provides diminishing returns? Or the opportunity cost is so low its fine
I mean, with all my buffs, Im hitting like 96
Am rather asking in general or its case by case varies from one team comp to another?

the actual nature of diminishing returns is wierd when it comes to crit. If you crit every hit your outright doing 200% damage on its own. But a drop of 10% rate, is a roughly 20% drop in dps. It's not a true drop, but is a question of avg damage gain vs loss
and if you have crit damage bonus, that theoretical loss could quickly add up to well over 50% or more
It's due to the fact if you have 90% chance, but again it's a 1 in 10 hits might not crit, but its rolls that chance with every hit. The actual avg breaks down to less then 9 crits out of 10 attacks
law of averages and all that jazz
your actually more likely to only hit 7-8 crits with 90% chance
which FEELS weird
but it kinda is the nature of dice rolls
the normal avg break point where crit damage and base damage become more of a focus for any crit setup is about 70%

But if you go all the way to 100% chance, any other boosts to base damage, crit damage, etc is just a flat out gain. Yes you could level off that law of average curve by going with 70% chance, and putting everything else in base damage. Which is the technically correct way to build crit setups. It's just easier to have 100% crit
